title: How do I pull a list of all my previous completed tasks and export that to excel for all of 2024?
excerpt: How do I pull a list of all my previous completed tasks and export that to excel for all of 2024?

posts.author: JoannRucker33913 (Joann)
posts.cookedHtml: How do I pull a list of all my previous completed tasks and export that to excel for all of 2024?

posts.author: JoannRucker33913 (Joann)
posts.cookedHtml: Just to note: I have tried exporting it to Excel, but it just sits there thinking for hours and won’t export.

posts.author: snowdive.tech (hello@snowdive.tech)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Joann,
if this is still an issue, you could try downloading all tasks via the Monday.com API and then save them as an .csv/xlsx.

posts.author: rooclown101 (RooclownTechno)
posts.cookedHtml:
1 Open Microsoft Outlook and then click on “Tasks” in the left-hand pane. Then click “Mail”, “Calendar”, “Contacts”, and finally select “Tasks”.

2 You should now only see the completed tasks. To do this, click on the “View” tab at the top, then click on “View Settings” in the drop-down menu, and finally select “Filter” in the dialog box.

On the Advanced tab, you should add two conditions for filtering your completed tasks

Condition 1: Show Completed - Field Status = “Completed.”

Condition 2: Only 2024 - Field Completed date = “Between 01/01/2024 and 12/31/2024”.

Click on the OK button to close the Filter dialog box. This will leave you with only the tasks from 2024 that have been completed.

3 After filtering your completed tasks for 2024, you will want to export your list of filtered tasks into an Excel spreadsheet. To do this:

Press Ctrl + A to select all of the filtered tasks

Press Ctrl + C to copy all of the filtered tasks to the clipboard.

Open Microsoft Excel.

Press Ctrl + V to paste all of the filtered tasks into a new worksheet.

The complete details of each task (including subject, start date, completed date, categories, etc.) will now be displayed in Excel.
